title: Skin
questions: What images spring to mind when you hear the word 'skin'?
How often do you think about your skin?
What is the function of skin?
What do you do to look after your skin?
Do you like the colour of your skin? (Why?)
Have you ever had any skin problems?
Why do people have different coloured skin?
Is beauty skin deep?
Have you ever escaped something by the skin of your teeth?

Martin Luther King Jr. said: "I have a dream that my four little children will one day live in a nation where they will not be judged by the color of their skin but by the content of their character." Has this dream come true?
What skincare products do you buy?
What's the best thing to keep your skin smooth?
What problems has skin colour caused in your country?
Do you believe ads that promise younger and healthier looking skin?
Some people want to tan their skin and some want to lighten their skin. Why is this?
Why do you think skin care for men has only recently become popular?
Do you worry about skin cancer?
Do you get dry or had skin anywhere on your body?
Have you ever been soaked to the skin?
Nietzsche: "The Earth has a skin and that skin has diseases, one of its diseases is called man." What do you think of this quote?
